perturbation.original_text: The Company agrees that it shall hold the 2020 Annual Meeting no later than July 8, 2020 and shall not cause or permit any delay, postponement or adjournment thereof, except for any adjournment solely due to a lack of quorum under the Company's Fourth Amended and Restated By-Laws (the "By-Laws").
perturbation.changed_text: The Company agrees that it shall hold the 2020 Annual Meeting no later than July 8, 2020, but the Company reserves the right to postpone or adjourn the meeting at its sole discretion, irrespective of quorum or any other condition.
perturbation.explanation: The original text strictly prohibits any delay or postponement of the 2020 Annual Meeting, except due to a lack of quorum. The modified text contradicts this by allowing the Company to postpone or adjourn the meeting at its sole discretion. This creates uncertainty about the meeting date, undermining the initial agreement and making it unclear whether MG Capital can rely on the meeting taking place by July 8, 2020, as previously agreed. The uncertainty is inserted with `but the Company reserves the right to postpone or adjourn the meeting at its sole discretion, irrespective of quorum or any other condition.`.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Company agrees that it shall hold the 2020 Annual Meeting no later than July 8, 2020, but the Company reserves the right to postpone or adjourn the meeting at its sole discretion, irrespective of quorum or any other condition.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original agreement regarding the 2020 Annual Meeting. The original text mandates the meeting to be held no later than July 8, 2020, with limited exceptions for quorum issues. The changed text introduces a conflicting statement granting the Company the right to postpone or adjourn the meeting at its sole discretion, irrespective of quorum or other conditions. This creates a structural flaw because the same clause is now in place with conflicting implications. This directly undermines the initial commitment and generates uncertainty regarding the meeting's occurrence as previously agreed, as per the provided Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: The MG Capital Parties hereby agree that during the Standstill Period, none of the MG Capital Parties nor any of their Affiliates and Associates will, and they will cause each of their Affiliates and Associates not to, as applicable, directly or indirectly, alone or in concert with others, in any manner, but expressly subject, in each case, to the provisions of Section 3(b) below: (i) fail to comply with all applicable laws and regulatory rules and obtain all applicable regulatory approvals, if and when acquiring, or offering, seeking or agreeing to acquire, by purchase or otherwise, or directing any third party in the acquisition of, any Common Stock or any securities convertible or exchangeable into or exercisable for Common Stock (collectively, "Company Securities"), or rights or options to acquire any Company Securities, or engaging in any swap instrument or derivative hedging transactions or other derivative agreements of any nature with respect to Company Securities;
perturbation.changed_text: The MG Capital Parties hereby agree that during the Standstill Period, none of the MG Capital Parties nor any of their Affiliates and Associates will, and they will cause each of their Affiliates and Associates not to, as applicable, directly or indirectly, alone or in concert with others, in any manner, but expressly subject, in each case, to the provisions of Section 3(b) below: (i) unless otherwise agreed to by the Company, fail to comply with all applicable laws and regulatory rules and obtain all applicable regulatory approvals, if and when acquiring, or offering, seeking or agreeing to acquire, by purchase or otherwise, or directing any third party in the acquisition of, any Common Stock or any securities convertible or exchangeable into or exercisable for Common Stock (collectively, "Company Securities"), or rights or options to acquire any Company Securities, or engaging in any swap instrument or derivative hedging transactions or other derivative agreements of any nature with respect to Company Securities;
perturbation.explanation: The original text says that MG Capital Parties must comply with all applicable laws, etc when acquiring company securities. However, the inserted perturbation changes this statement to say `unless otherwise agreed to by the Company, fail to comply with all applicable laws and regulatory rules` This would create an in-text contradiction, as it is implying that the Company can allow for MG Capital Parties to violate applicable laws and regulatory rules. The uncertainty is inserted with `unless otherwise agreed to by the Company`.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The MG Capital Parties hereby agree that during the Standstill Period, none of the MG Capital Parties nor any of their Affiliates and Associates will, and they will cause each of their Affiliates and Associates not to, as applicable, directly or indirectly, alone or in concert with others, in any manner, but expressly subject, in each case, to the provisions of Section 3(b) below: (i) unless otherwise agreed to by the Company, fail to comply with all applicable laws and regulatory rules and obtain all applicable regulatory approvals, if and when acquiring, or offering, seeking or agreeing to acquire, by purchase or otherwise, or directing any third party in the acquisition of, any Common Stock or any securities convertible or exchangeable into or exercisable for Common Stock (collectively, "Company Securities"), or rights or options to acquire any Company Securities, or engaging in any swap instrument or derivative hedging transactions or other derivative agreements of any nature with respect to Company Securities;
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. The original text states MG Capital Parties must comply with all laws when acquiring company securities. The change allows them to fail to comply with laws if the Company agrees. The Contradicted Text is the same text with the changed content. This creates a direct conflict, as the revised clause implies the company can authorize violations of laws, which is inherently contradictory to the original requirement of compliance. The addition of 'unless otherwise agreed to by the Company' directly contradicts the need to 'comply with all applicable laws and regulatory rules'.

perturbation.original_text: Subject to applicable law, each of the Parties covenants and agrees that, during the Standstill Period, neither Party nor any of its subsidiaries, Affiliates, successors, assigns, principals, partners, members, general partners, officers, key employees or directors (collectively, "Representatives"), shall in any way, directly or indirectly, in any capacity or manner, whether written or oral, electronically or otherwise (including, without limitation, in a television, radio, internet, newspaper, magazine interview, or otherwise through the press, media, analysts or other persons or in any document or report filed with the SEC), publicly disparage, impugn, make ad hominem attacks on or otherwise defame or slander or make, express, transmit, speak, write, verbalize or otherwise publicly communicate in any way (or cause, further, assist, solicit, encourage, support or participate in any of the foregoing), any public communication or statement of any kind, whether verbal, in writing, electronically transferred or otherwise, that might reasonably be construed to be disparage, derogate or impugn, the other Party or such other Party's Representatives (including any current officer or director of a Party or a Parties' subsidiaries who no longer serves in such capacity following the execution of this Agreement), employees, stockholders (solely in their capacity as stockholders of the applicable Party), or any of their businesses, products or services, in any manner that would reasonably be expected to damage the business, or reputation of the other Party or of its Representatives (including former officers and directors), directors (or former directors), employees, stockholders (solely in their capacity as stockholders of the applicable Party); provided that, with respect to any litigation, arbitration or other proceeding between the Parties, nothing in this Section 5 shall prevent either Party from disclosing any facts or circumstances with respect to any such litigation, arbitration or other proceeding.
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to applicable law, each of the Parties covenants and agrees that, during the Standstill Period, neither Party nor any of its subsidiaries, Affiliates, successors, assigns, principals, partners, members, general partners, officers, key employees or directors (collectively, "Representatives"), shall in any way, directly or indirectly, in any capacity or manner, whether written or oral, electronically or otherwise (including, without limitation, in a television, radio, internet, newspaper, magazine interview, or otherwise through the press, media, analysts or other persons or in any document or report filed with the SEC), publicly disparage, impugn, make ad hominem attacks on or otherwise defame or slander or make, express, transmit, speak, write, verbalize or otherwise publicly communicate in any way (or cause, further, assist, solicit, encourage, support or participate in any of the foregoing), any public communication or statement of any kind, whether verbal, in writing, electronically transferred or otherwise, that might reasonably be construed to be disparage, derogate or impugn, the other Party or such other Party's Representatives (including any current officer or director of a Party or a Parties' subsidiaries who no longer serves in such capacity following the execution of this Agreement), employees, stockholders (solely in their capacity as stockholders of the applicable Party), or any of their businesses, products or services, in any manner that would reasonably be expected to damage the business, or reputation of the other Party or of its Representatives (including former officers and directors), directors (or former directors), employees, stockholders (solely in their capacity as stockholders of the applicable Party), notwithstanding the truthfulness of such communication; provided that, with respect to any litigation, arbitration or other proceeding between the Parties, nothing in this Section 5 shall prevent either Party from disclosing any facts or circumstances with respect to any such litigation, arbitration or other proceeding.
perturbation.explanation: The original text prevents the parties from disparaging each other. With the inclusion of the `notwithstanding the truthfulness of such communication`, it creates an in-text contradiction as the Parties are not allowed to comment on the other Party in a public setting even if the statement is true. It creates uncertainty because under the standard legal proceedings if the truth is told, there are often protections against claims. This eliminates this protections due to the change.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to applicable law, each of the Parties covenants and agrees that, during the Standstill Period, neither Party nor any of its subsidiaries, Affiliates, successors, assigns, principals, partners, members, general partners, officers, key employees or directors (collectively, "Representatives"), shall in any way, directly or indirectly, in any capacity or manner, whether written or oral, electronically or otherwise (including, without limitation, in a television, radio, internet, newspaper, magazine interview, or otherwise through the press, media, analysts or other persons or in any document or report filed with the SEC), publicly disparage, impugn, make ad hominem attacks on or otherwise defame or slander or make, express, transmit, speak, write, verbalize or otherwise publicly communicate in any way (or cause, further, assist, solicit, encourage, support or participate in any of the foregoing), any public communication or statement of any kind, whether verbal, in writing, electronically transferred or otherwise, that might reasonably be construed to be disparage, derogate or impugn, the other Party or such other Party's Representatives (including any current officer or director of a Party or a Parties' subsidiaries who no longer serves in such capacity following the execution of this Agreement), employees, stockholders (solely in their capacity as stockholders of the applicable Party), or any of their businesses, products or services, in any manner that would reasonably be expected to damage the business, or reputation of the other Party or of its Representatives (including former officers and directors), directors (or former directors), employees, stockholders (solely in their capacity as stockholders of the applicable Party), notwithstanding the truthfulness of such communication; provided that, with respect to any litigation, arbitration or other proceeding between the Parties, nothing in this Section 5 shall prevent either Party from disclosing any facts or circumstances with respect to any such litigation, arbitration or other proceeding.
perturbation.justification: The addition of "notwithstanding the truthfulness of such communication" in the changed text directly contradicts the implied protection that truthful statements usually have in legal proceedings. This creates a situation where a party could be penalized for making a true statement, which introduces uncertainty and conflict within the agreement regarding permissible communications. The changed text and contradicted text are the same in this instance, which highlights that the modification within the text itself is what creates the contradiction.
